characters.description: Fifteen and increasingly angry, Harry begins the year isolated and frustrated: haunted by Cedric's death, cut off from news, and disbelieved when he insists that Voldemort has returned. Branded a liar by the wizarding press and the Ministry, he struggles with his temper and a growing, disturbing link to Voldemort's mind, while shouldering more than any boy his age should have to.
characters.name: Harry Potter

characters.description: Harry's steadfast friend, staying with his family at the headquarters of a secret anti-Voldemort society. Made a Gryffindor prefect this year alongside Hermione, he grows into a little more responsibility while remaining loyal to Harry through the isolation and suspicion Harry faces.
characters.name: Ron Weasley

characters.description: Harry and Ron's clever, level-headed friend, who becomes a Gryffindor prefect and the strategist behind the students' resistance to the Ministry's grip on the school. She persuades Harry to secretly teach his classmates real defensive magic and manages his temper with patience and sense.
characters.name: Hermione Granger

characters.description: Harry's godfather, cleared in no one's eyes but Harry's and still a wanted fugitive, who shelters the secret society in his gloomy ancestral home. Reckless and restless at being confined and useless, he is the closest thing to a parent Harry has, and chafes bitterly at being unable to protect him openly.
characters.name: Sirius Black

characters.description: A young, cheerful Auror and member of the secret society guarding Harry, able to change her appearance at will. Clumsy but warm, she lightens the grim mood of the society's headquarters and helps escort Harry safely to it.
characters.name: Nymphadora Tonks

characters.description: A Ministry official, sweet-voiced and fond of pink and kittens but coldly authoritarian, who appears first at Harry's disciplinary hearing and is then installed as the Defence Against the Dark Arts teacher. She refuses to teach any practical magic, punishes dissent with cruel detentions, and steadily seizes control of the school on the Ministry's behalf.
characters.name: Dolores Umbridge

characters.description: A dreamy, serenely odd student in Ginny's year, whom Harry meets on the train. She believes in many things others mock, wears strange homemade jewellery, and speaks her mind without embarrassment, but she is loyal, unafraid, and more perceptive than she first appears.
characters.name: Luna Lovegood

characters.description: Ron's younger sister, no longer the shy girl of earlier years but confident, quick-tongued, and unafraid to stand up to her brothers or to Harry. She becomes a committed member of the students' secret defence group and a capable young witch in her own right.
characters.name: Ginny Weasley

characters.description: Harry's clumsy, good-hearted classmate, who joins the secret defence group and works harder at it than anyone. He lives with his grandmother; his parents were tortured into madness by Voldemort's followers, a loss that gives his determination a quiet, private weight.
characters.name: Neville Longbottom

characters.description: The Hogwarts headmaster, now openly opposed by a Ministry that fears he is raising an army, and stripped of his official positions for insisting Voldemort has returned. This year he avoids Harry's eyes and keeps him at a strange distance, for reasons Harry cannot understand and finds deeply hurtful.
characters.name: Albus Dumbledore

characters.description: The Potions master, tasked by Dumbledore with teaching Harry to close his mind against Voldemort's intrusions. The lessons go badly, poisoned by their mutual loathing.
characters.name: Severus Snape

characters.description: A fanatically devoted follower of Voldemort and one of his most dangerous, who escapes Azkaban in a mass breakout. Cruel, unhinged, and proud of her loyalty, she is Sirius Black's cousin and was among those who tortured Neville Longbottom's parents into madness.
characters.name: Bellatrix Lestrange
